title: Leagues & commissioner
depends: game · golf-data · scoring · payments · accounts · web-shell

rows.value: League lifecycle (create → pay → invite → join → approve), the league pages, the active-league cookie, and commissioner tools + audit log

bigIdea.label: Big idea
bigIdea.line: Membership **is** the permission system — every other gameplay domain answers "may this user act here?" by calling into leagues.
bigIdea.sub: `resolveLeagueMemberId` and `assertLeagueActive` (src/server/services/league.ts) are imported by the pick and dashboard services; the `leagueMemberId` they return is the identity key that picks, standings, and earnings all hang off. Everyone is lazily auto-enrolled in the global league (`ppgl-global`), so the guard doubles as onboarding.

blocks.title: Components
blocks.intro: Six units: one server trio holding all the business logic, four UI surfaces, and a tiny cookie-based context layer that the whole app reads.

cards.name: league service
cards.purpose: The router → service → repository trio. All lifecycle rules live in `services/league.ts`: create/join with race-safe transactions, the member state machine, payment gating (`PENDING` leagues block everything), promo redemption, audit-log assembly, and abandoned-league cleanup.

cards.name: create & checkout
cards.purpose: `/league/create` — one client form that handles the $15 PayPal path (league born `PENDING`, activated on capture), the promo path (free → instantly `PAID`, partial → discounted checkout), and hands off to `/league/created` with the invite link.

cards.name: join & approval
cards.purpose: The invite funnel: `/league/join?code=…` previews the league (public, works signed-out), joins or requests to join, and the commissioner's inline approve/reject queue on the league page.

cards.name: league pages
cards.purpose: `/leagues` index and the league home `/league/[id]` — a large server component that fans out to scoring, game, and golf-data for the weekly view + season standings — plus per-league `/rules` (settings-aware how-to-play) and `/settings`.

cards.name: league context
cards.purpose: Which league am I looking at? A one-year `ppgl-active-league` cookie read by every league-scoped page via `getActiveLeagueId`, written only through server actions — by the nav `LeagueSwitcher` or silently by `SyncLeagueContext` when you visit `/league/[id]`.

cards.name: commissioner tools
cards.purpose: The commissioner-mode overlay for acting on *other members'* picks: a CSS toggle plus add/edit/remove widgets that call the game domain's `pick.commissioner*` procedures — every action lands in this domain's `CommissionerAction` audit log.

blocks.title: Internal architecture
blocks.intro: One thin tRPC router in front of one fat service. The service is also entered from the side — by the PayPal webhook, by the cron sync's cleanup, and by other domains' services importing its guards directly.

diagram.d2:
direction: right
"member": { class: actor }
"league pages": "app/league/*"
"leagueRouter": "routers/league.ts"
"league service": { class: changed }
"league repository": "repositories/league.ts"
"Postgres": { class: store }
"PayPal": { class: external }
"active-league cookie": { class: store }
"member" -> "league pages"
"league pages" -> "leagueRouter": tRPC
"leagueRouter" -> "league service"
"league service" -> "league repository"
"league repository" -> "Postgres"
"league service" -> "PayPal": checkout + capture
"PayPal webhook" -> "league service": handleWebhookPayment
"cron sync" -> "league service": cleanupAbandonedLeagues
"pick + dashboard services" -> "league service": resolveLeagueMemberId
"LeagueSwitcher" -> "active-league cookie": server action
"league pages" -> "active-league cookie": SyncLeagueContext
"commissioner tools" -> "pick router": commissioner picks
diagram.caption: `league service` is the hub: fronted by the router, but also called directly by the payments webhook, the golf-data cron, and the game domain's services. The commissioner UI bypasses it entirely — those mutations belong to the pick router.

blocks.callout: Note the two side doors into the service: `handleWebhookPayment` (from `src/app/api/paypal/webhook/route.ts`) and `cleanupAbandonedLeagues` (from `src/server/services/cron-sync.ts`). Neither goes through tRPC.

components.name: league service
detail: `routers/league.ts` is deliberately thin — 16 procedures that only zod-validate and delegate (league name 3–50 chars, `maxGolferUses` 1–3, everything else booleans and ids). All 16 are `protectedProcedure` except `league.preview`, which is public so an invite link can render a league preview to signed-out visitors.

`services/league.ts` holds the real rules. Creation inserts League + commissioner LeagueMember + LeagueMemberSeason atomically via `withTransaction` (shared helper `insertLeagueWithMember`). Joins are race-safe twice over: the 20-member cap (`MAX_LEAGUE_SIZE`) is re-counted *inside* the transaction to kill the TOCTOU window, and a concurrent double-join is caught by the `@@unique([userId, leagueId])` constraint then re-queried. The same catch-requery pattern makes `ensureGlobalLeagueMembership` idempotent — global-league membership is created lazily the first time any league-scoped call runs.

Member lifecycle: joining an open league → `ACTIVE`; with `requireApproval` on → `PENDING` (no season row until approved, so pending members never appear in standings). `approveMember` re-checks the cap, flips to `ACTIVE`, inserts the season row, and logs an `APPROVE_MEMBER` audit action. `rejectMember` hard-deletes the row — the code comments that no audit entry is possible because `CommissionerAction.targetMemberId` has an FK to the member being deleted. `removeMember` soft-removes (`REMOVED`) but scrubs the member's planned picks, season rows, and earnings adjustments; locked picks survive. Removed members can rejoin (back through approval if required).

Payment gating: privately created leagues are born `paymentStatus: PENDING` and inert — `assertLeagueActive` rejects picks and `joinLeague` rejects joins until PayPal capture (client `captureOrder` or webhook `handleWebhookPayment`) flips them to `PAID` via `confirmPayment`, which also redeems any deferred partial-discount promo. `cleanupAbandonedLeagues` deletes `PENDING` leagues older than 48h; the repo re-checks `paymentStatus` inside its delete transaction to avoid racing a late webhook.

diagrams.d2:
direction: right
join -> ACTIVE: open league
join -> PENDING: requireApproval
PENDING -> ACTIVE: approveMember
PENDING -> deleted: rejectMember
ACTIVE -> REMOVED: removeMember
REMOVED -> ACTIVE: rejoin open
REMOVED -> PENDING: rejoin w. approval
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  J[join via invite] -->|open league| A[ACTIVE]
  J -->|requireApproval| P[PENDING]
  P -->|approveMember| A
  P -->|rejectMember| D[row deleted]
  A -->|removeMember| R[REMOVED]
  R -->|rejoin open| A
  R -->|rejoin w. approval| P
diagrams.caption: `LeagueMemberStatus` transitions in `services/league.ts`. Only `ACTIVE` members get a `LeagueMemberSeason` row — which is why pending members are invisible to standings.

files.name: server/routers/league.ts
files.desc: 16 thin tRPC procedures — zod input validation, then one service call each. `preview` is the lone `publicProcedure`.

files.name: server/services/league.ts
files.desc: All business logic: create/join/approve/remove, payment gating + PayPal orchestration, promo validation, audit-log assembly, cleanup. The only file other domains import guards from.

files.name: server/repositories/league.ts
files.desc: Every league/member/audit query (Kysely). Functions take an optional `executor` so the service can pass a transaction. Also hosts cross-table cleanup deletes (`deletePlannedPicks`, `deleteEarningsAdjustments`) used by `removeMember`.

exports.name: leagueRouter
exports.desc: Mounted as `league.*` in `_app.ts`; the domain's entire tRPC surface.

exports.name: resolveLeagueMemberId
exports.desc: userId + leagueId → active `leagueMemberId`, or throws. The cross-domain authz primitive (imported by pick + dashboard services).

exports.name: assertLeagueActive
exports.desc: Rejects any mutation against a `PENDING` (unpaid) league; global league always passes.

exports.name: ensureGlobalLeagueMembership
exports.desc: Lazy, idempotent, race-safe auto-enrollment in `ppgl-global`.

exports.name: joinLeague
exports.desc: Invite-code join with size cap, approval flow, and removed-member reactivation.

exports.name: getAuditLog
exports.desc: Reads `CommissionerAction` rows, parses the `details` JSON, and batch-resolves golfer/tournament names via golf-data repos.

exports.name: handleWebhookPayment
exports.desc: Verifies the PayPal webhook signature then confirms payment — the server-to-server path when the client capture never lands.

exports.name: cleanupAbandonedLeagues
exports.desc: Deletes 48h-old unpaid leagues; invoked from golf-data's `cron-sync.ts`.

exports.name: createCommissionerAction
exports.desc: (repo) Audit-log insert — also called by game's pick service and admin's service when they act on members.

connections.body: [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) — `services/pick.ts` and `services/dashboard.ts` import `resolveLeagueMemberId` / `assertLeagueActive`; the pick service also reads `getMaxGolferUses` and writes audit rows via `createCommissionerAction`.

[payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html) — `paypalProvider` (`lib/paypal.ts`) for checkout/capture/webhook-verify, `LEAGUE_CREATION_PRICE` (`lib/payment.ts`), and `repositories/promo-code.ts` for promo lookup; redemption (`redeemPromoCode`) lives in this domain's repo.

[golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html) — `golferRepo.findNamesByIds` / `tournamentRepo.findNamesByIds` to humanize audit entries, and `getCurrentSeason` (`lib/season.ts`) for season rows.

[scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) and [admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html) — leaderboard/earnings-adjustment services verify membership via `leagueRepo` lookups; admin lists a user's commissioned leagues.

components.name: create & checkout
detail: `CreateLeagueForm` is a single client component handling three payment paths. No promo: `league.create` makes a `PENDING` league, then `league.createCheckoutSession` fetches a PayPal order id and the form swaps to an embedded `PayPalButtons` checkout (`@paypal/react-paypal-js`); on approval it calls `league.captureOrder` and routes to `/league/created`. Free promo: `league.createWithPromo` redeems the code inside the creation transaction and the league is born `PAID` — straight to the success page. Partial-discount promo: league is `PENDING` with `promoCodeId` set, checkout runs at the discounted price, and redemption is deferred to payment confirmation.

The form also collects the rule settings that the rest of the game reads back later: format (`maxGolferUses` — "One and Done" vs "Double Use"), `burnStalePicks`, `showPicksBeforeLock`, and `requireApproval`. `/league/created` reads `?id=&code=` and shows the copyable invite URL (`/league/join?code=…`) — the commissioner's first job is distribution.

diagrams.d2:
direction: right
"create form" -> "league.create": no promo
"league.create" -> "PENDING league"
"PENDING league" -> "PayPal checkout": createCheckoutSession
"PayPal checkout" -> "league.captureOrder"
"league.captureOrder" -> "PAID league"
"create form" -> "league.createWithPromo": promo code
"league.createWithPromo" -> "PAID league": free promo
"league.createWithPromo" -> "PayPal checkout": partial discount
"PENDING league" -> "deleted": 48h cleanup
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  F["create form"] -->|no promo| C["league.create"]
  C --> PEND["PENDING league"]
  PEND -->|createCheckoutSession| PP["PayPal checkout"]
  PP --> CAP["league.captureOrder"]
  CAP --> PAID["PAID league"]
  F -->|promo code| WP["league.createWithPromo"]
  WP -->|free promo| PAID
  WP -->|partial discount| PP
  PEND -->|48h cleanup| DEL["deleted"]
diagrams.caption: Three routes to a live league. A `PENDING` league is fully inert — no joins, no picks — until capture; abandon it and the cron deletes it after 48 hours.

files.name: app/league/create/page.tsx
files.desc: Server shell: `auth.me` gate → renders the client form.

files.name: app/league/create/create-league-form.tsx
files.desc: The three-path form: settings + promo validation (`league.validatePromoCode`) + embedded `PayPalButtons` checkout. Reads env var name `NEXT_PUBLIC_PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID`.

files.name: app/league/created/page.tsx
files.desc: Success page; redirects home if `?id`/`?code` are missing.

files.name: app/league/created/league-created-client.tsx
files.desc: Invite-link display + clipboard copy, CTA into the new league.

exports.name: CreateLeagueForm
exports.desc: Client component owning the whole create→pay state machine (`pendingLeague` state flips it into checkout mode).

exports.name: LeagueCreatedClient
exports.desc: Copyable invite URL built from `window.location.origin`.

connections.body: [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html) — the PayPal JS SDK (`PayPalScriptProvider`/`PayPalButtons`); server-side order handling goes through `paypalProvider`.

components.name: join & approval
detail: `/league/join?code=…` is the invite landing page and it works signed-out: the server page calls public `league.preview` for name + member count, then branches on auth and membership state. Signed-out visitors get sign-up/sign-in links that carry `?invite=<code>` through the auth flow; existing members get a "Go to League" shortcut; unpaid leagues show an "awaiting payment" notice instead of a join button.

`JoinLeagueClient` fires `league.join` and renders the outcome: `ACTIVE` → "You're In!" with a How-to-Play CTA; `PENDING` → "Request Sent" interstitial. On the approval side, `PendingMembers` (rendered on the league page only for the commissioner of a `requireApproval` league) polls `league.pendingMembers` and mutates `league.approveMember` / `league.rejectMember`, then invalidates the query *and* `router.refresh()`es so the server-rendered header badge and standings update together. Meanwhile a pending member visiting `/league/[id]` sees a blocked interstitial that points them at the global league while they wait.

files.name: app/league/join/page.tsx
files.desc: Server page: `league.preview` by invite code, resolves the visitor's `memberStatus` via `league.myLeagues`, handles invalid/missing codes.

files.name: app/league/join/join-league-client.tsx
files.desc: Join button + all outcome states (joined / pending / already member / unpaid league / signed-out).

files.name: app/league/[id]/pending-members.tsx
files.desc: Commissioner approval queue: approve/reject with shared disabled state and a combined query-invalidate + server refresh.

files.name: app/league/[id]/invite-link-copy.tsx
files.desc: Read-only invite-URL input + clipboard copy, shown in the league page's Invite Link card.

exports.name: JoinLeagueClient
exports.desc: Client join flow around `league.join`.

exports.name: PendingMembers
exports.desc: Approve/reject queue over `league.pendingMembers`.

exports.name: InviteLinkCopy
exports.desc: Same copy pattern as the created page, reused on the league home.

connections.body: [accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html) — signed-out joiners route through `/sign-up?invite=<code>` / `/sign-in?invite=<code>` so the invite survives authentication.

components.name: league pages
detail: `/league/[id]/page.tsx` is the biggest page in the app — a server component that fans out in `Promise.all` to five other-domain procedures: `leaderboard.byLeague` (season standings), `pick.leagueCurrentWeek` (this week's picks), `league.auditLog`, `tournament.schedule`, and `leaderboard.weeklyStandings` when a `?week=` param selects a past tournament. A local `leagueHref` helper threads `page`/`twPage`/`week` params through every pagination and navigation link, and `WeekSwipeWrapper` adds mobile swipe-to-navigate between weeks on top of the arrow links.

The page renders differently by viewer: pending members get an interstitial, commissioners get the pending-members queue and an unpaid-league banner with a "Complete Payment" link back into checkout, private-league members get the invite-link card and the commissioner-actions audit log. The inline pick table swaps between a "planned" view (before lock, only if `showPicksBeforeLock` or picks are the viewer's own — enforced server-side in game's service) and a live/final earnings view.

`/league/[id]/rules` is a settings-aware how-to-play: its withdrawal, visibility, and joining sections change copy based on the league's `burnStalePicks` / `showPicksBeforeLock` / `requireApproval` flags. `/league/[id]/settings` lets the commissioner edit those same flags via `league.updateSettings` (non-commissioners see a read-only form) and manage the roster — `LeagueMembers` confirms then fires `league.removeMember`, warning that planned picks will be deleted.

files.name: app/leagues/page.tsx
files.desc: League index: global league pinned on top, private leagues below with Pending Payment badges, Create League CTA.

files.name: app/league/[id]/page.tsx
files.desc: The league home: weekly view + standings orchestrator; branches for pending members, unpaid leagues, commissioners.

files.name: app/league/[id]/week-swipe-wrapper.tsx
files.desc: Touch swipe → `router.push` between prev/next week URLs (50px threshold, ignores vertical scrolls).

files.name: app/league/[id]/audit-log-collapsible.tsx
files.desc: Renders audit entries as sentences ("X changed a pick for Y"), parsing the `details` JSON per action type; collapsed to 5.

files.name: app/league/[id]/rules/page.tsx
files.desc: How-to-play with screenshots, glossary, and rule cards that mirror the league's actual settings.

files.name: app/league/[id]/settings/page.tsx
files.desc: Server shell: fetches `league.details` + (commissioner-only) `league.members`.

files.name: app/league/[id]/settings/league-settings-form.tsx
files.desc: Name/format/toggles form → `league.updateSettings`; inputs disabled unless commissioner.

files.name: app/league/[id]/settings/league-members.tsx
files.desc: Roster with confirm-to-remove rows → `league.removeMember`.

exports.name: WeekSwipeWrapper
exports.desc: Mobile week navigation wrapper around the weekly section.

exports.name: AuditLogCollapsible
exports.desc: Human-readable commissioner-action feed.

exports.name: LeagueSettingsForm
exports.desc: Commissioner settings editor (same fields as the create form).

exports.name: LeagueMembers
exports.desc: Commissioner roster management.

connections.body: [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) — `leaderboard.byLeague` + `leaderboard.weeklyStandings`, and renders its `WeeklyStandingsTable` component for past weeks.
[game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) — `pick.leagueCurrentWeek`, `pick.myPickForTournament`, `pick.remainingPool`, and embeds its `LeagueThisWeekPicker` for inline picking.
[golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html) — `tournament.schedule` for week navigation, `GolferAvatar` headshots, golfer/tournament links.
[accounts](../domain-accounts/domain-accounts.html) — every page starts with `auth.me` and redirects to `/sign-in`.

components.name: league context
detail: The active league is a single httpOnly cookie (`ppgl-active-league`, 1-year max-age) managed by two server actions in `lib/league-context.ts`. `getActiveLeagueId` validates the cookie against the user's actual leagues (falling back to the first league) and honors a `searchParams` override for deep links — it deliberately never *writes* during render, because Next.js only allows cookie writes in server actions and route handlers.

Two writers exist: `LeagueSwitcher`, the nav-bar `<select>` (rendered by web-shell's `nav-bar.tsx` from `league.myLeagues`), which sets the cookie in a transition then either pushes to the new league's page or refreshes in place — it also smuggles a "+ Create League" option into the dropdown; and `SyncLeagueContext`, a render-nothing client component mounted on `/league/[id]` that syncs the cookie once on mount, so simply visiting a league makes it your active one. Dashboard, season, tournament, and member pages all read the same cookie to scope their queries.

files.name: lib/league-context.ts
files.desc: `"use server"` cookie accessors; read validates against the caller's league list, write is server-action-only.

files.name: components/league/league-switcher.tsx
files.desc: Nav dropdown: set cookie → navigate or refresh; hides itself when the user has ≤1 league.

files.name: components/league/sync-league-context.tsx
files.desc: Mount-once effect that calls `setActiveLeague(leagueId)` from the client so it executes as a server action.

exports.name: getActiveLeagueId
exports.desc: Cookie → validated active league id, with searchParam override for invite/deep links.

exports.name: setActiveLeague
exports.desc: Server action writing the httpOnly cookie.

exports.name: LeagueSwitcher
exports.desc: The app-wide league selector.

exports.name: SyncLeagueContext
exports.desc: Silent cookie sync on league-page visits.

connections.body: [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) and [scoring](../domain-scoring/domain-scoring.html) — dashboard, season, tournament, and member pages read `getActiveLeagueId` to scope their data fetches.

components.name: commissioner tools
detail: These widgets let a commissioner fix *someone else's* pick — the escape hatch for "my buddy forgot to pick before lock." `CommissionerToggle` is pure DOM: it flips a `data-commissioner-active` attribute on the nearest `[data-commissioner-container]`, and CSS reveals the per-row action buttons. No server state; commissioner-ness was already decided server-side by whichever page rendered the toggle.

`CommissionerPickActions` (edit/remove an existing pick) and `CommissionerAddPick` (add a missing one) share a pattern: local golfer typeahead over a pre-fetched list, a live `pick.commissionerValidate` query surfacing warnings (already used, exhausted uses — exhaustion hard-blocks the Add button), then `pick.commissionerUpdate` / `pick.commissionerRemove` / `pick.commissionerAdd`. Those mutations live in the game domain's pick service, which enforces commissioner authorization again server-side and writes the `CommissionerAction` rows this domain's audit log displays. The components are mounted from game-domain surfaces: the season page (`app/season/[userId]/page.tsx`) and the planner timeline.

files.name: components/commissioner/commissioner-toggle.tsx
files.desc: CSS-only mode switch via a data attribute; `aria-pressed` button.

files.name: components/commissioner/commissioner-pick-actions.tsx
files.desc: Edit/remove flow with typeahead, validation warnings, confirm step; full query invalidation on success.

files.name: components/commissioner/commissioner-add-pick.tsx
files.desc: Add-pick flow; blocks submission when the golfer's uses are exhausted.

exports.name: CommissionerToggle
exports.desc: Enter/exit commissioner mode.

exports.name: CommissionerPickActions
exports.desc: Edit or remove a member's pick.

exports.name: CommissionerAddPick
exports.desc: Add a pick on a member's behalf.

connections.body: [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) — `pick.commissionerValidate`, `pick.commissionerAdd`, `pick.commissionerUpdate`, `pick.commissionerRemove`; the pick service writes the audit rows via this domain's `createCommissionerAction`.
[game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) — `app/season/[userId]/page.tsx` and `components/planner/planner-timeline.tsx` render these widgets on commissioner-visible rows.
Audit entries surfaced by [`AuditLogCollapsible`](#c-pages) on the league home.

blocks.title: Data it owns
blocks.intro: The membership graph and its paper trail. All writes go through `repositories/league.ts`.

rows.name: League
rows.desc: Name, unique `inviteCode`, season, `type` (`GLOBAL`/`PRIVATE`), `commissionerId`, the rule flags (`maxGolferUses`, `burnStalePicks`, `showPicksBeforeLock`, `requireApproval`), and payment state (`paymentStatus`, `paymentSessionId`, `promoCodeId`).

rows.name: LeagueMember
rows.desc: user ↔ league with `status` (`ACTIVE`/`PENDING`/`REMOVED`) and `@@unique([userId, leagueId])` — the constraint the join/global-enroll race handling leans on. Its `id` (`leagueMemberId`) is the key picks and earnings hang off.

rows.name: LeagueMemberSeason
rows.desc: Per-member-per-season row created here on join/approval; the `totalEarnings`/`totalPicks`/`totalMissedCuts` rollups on it are *written* by scoring's earnings pipeline.

rows.name: CommissionerAction
rows.desc: Append-only audit log: `action` string + `details` JSON (golfer/tournament ids resolved to names at read time). Written here (approve/remove) and by game's pick service + admin.

blocks.note: `PromoCode` is referenced (`League.promoCodeId`) and *redeemed* here (`redeemPromoCode` guards uses/expiry in SQL), but the model and its CRUD belong to [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html). `Pick` belongs to [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html) — this domain only deletes `PLANNED` picks when removing a member.

blocks.title: Seams
blocks.intro: One tRPC router out; two service functions that act as the app-wide membership guard; five domains leaned on.

exposes.note: Leagues for the nav switcher and join-page membership check; auto-enrolls the caller in the global league first.
Public — invite-link landing preview (name, member count, paid state).
Checks active/uses/expiry, returns discount + final price.
Free promo → `PAID` immediately; partial → `PENDING` + discounted checkout.
Commissioner-only; PayPal order for an unpaid league.
Client-side capture path; verifies the order belongs to the league.
Invite-code join — cap-checked, approval-aware, reactivates `REMOVED` members.
League + member count + caller's `memberStatus`; membership required.
Commissioner-only edit of name and rule flags.
Flips `PENDING`→`ACTIVE`, creates the season row, logs `APPROVE_MEMBER`.
Hard-deletes the pending membership (no audit row possible — FK).
Active roster with usernames; any member may read.
Commissioner-only soft remove + scrub of planned picks/season/adjustments; logs `REMOVE_MEMBER`.
Commissioner-action feed with names resolved from golf-data.

blocks.note: Beyond tRPC, the load-bearing seam is direct service imports: `resolveLeagueMemberId` / `assertLeagueActive` (used by [game](../domain-game/domain-game.html)'s pick + dashboard services), `handleWebhookPayment` (called from [payments](../domain-payments/domain-payments.html)' webhook route), `cleanupAbandonedLeagues` (called from [golf-data](../domain-golf-data/domain-golf-data.html)'s cron sync), and repo-level `createCommissionerAction` / `getMaxGolferUses` (game + [admin](../domain-admin/domain-admin.html)). The dependency arrows point both ways: leagues guards them, they call it.
